  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager",
  "description": "VisionSpring is creating access to affordable eyewear, everywhere. Clear vision creates opportunities for increased learning,&nbsp;&nbsp;work, safety, civic participation, and quality of life. As a social enterprise, social change motivates us first. Our focus on sales and revenue targets serves and advances our mission objective&mdash;to increase functioning, productivity, and income earning potential for our low-income consumers by correcting refractive error and presbyopia with eyeglasses. We are working to transform the systemic dysfunction of an optical market that has failed to deliver eyeglasses&mdash;a 700-year-old technology&mdash;to 2.7 billion consumers in need of vision correction, most of whom live on less than $4 a day.&nbsp;

We serve low-income consumers, not as beneficiaries but as customers. Our customers expect a high-quality and affordable product, and they will spend limited discretionary income for the immediate and tangible benefits of vision correction. By selling eyeglasses, we awaken new demand and seed a viable market. By selling new eyeglasses we are able to serve four times as many people per dollar input than the alternative of donating recycled ones. To efficiently scale, we must deliver each new unit at a lower cost. We run our business on both sales metrics and social impact measures. We use a range of organizational forms and practices to get the&nbsp;&nbsp;job done. We believe in scaling our impact, not our organization. We are a lean team of doers.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager

Job Location: Nigeria

Description


	We are seeking a Business Development Manager for Nigeria to join our team and build strategic partnerships with a growing portfolio of dynamic, sophisticated funders. Reporting to the Country Manager -Nigeria this role will lead fundraising and business development efforts with Nigeria corporations, governments, and philanthropic investors (individual and foundation). This role will also support global fundraising and business development grants directed to Africa but originating in the global North.&nbsp;
	The person in this role will work as part of an entrepreneurial team to raise the philanthropic capital needed to augment our earned revenue and will collaborate closely with program teams in all African countries where VisionSpring is active as well as program and business development teams based in the U.S. and Asia. &nbsp;Based in Lagos, Nigeria, he/she will report to the Country Manager based in Lagos Nigeria.&nbsp;
	This role is a contract-based, full-time position for the initial period of one year. As with other contractors with whom we have worked for many years, we expect the successful candidates to be willing to be long-term contractors for VisionSpring.


Job Responsibilities
Sales Management, Donor Engagement &amp; Fundraising:


	Drive strong sales growth by actively promoting and selling eyeglasses through retail, outreach, and institutional channels, consistently meeting and exceeding monthly and quarterly targets.
	Ensure prudent management of client credit lines, maintaining a healthy balance between sales growth and risk, while guaranteeing timely payment from all creditors and adherence to credit policies.
	Cultivate and nurture a portfolio of funders, including governments, institutional foundations, corporate and individual donors, by expanding long-term relationships with VisionSpring&rsquo;s philanthropic investors and building new partnerships with prospective donors.
	Support the continual improvement of our organizational donor stewardship practices to maintain the effectiveness and integrity of VisionSpring collaborations. Proactively communicate with donors and look for ways to engage philanthropic investors in order to keep them connected to our work.
	Ensure donors receive timely, comprehensive, and accurate reports.
	Prepare thoughtful and what can be complex and multi-layered proposals and requests for renewal funding, working collaboratively with program/field staff on proposal writing, objectives, indicator development, and budgeting.
	Develop and monitor the opportunity pipeline and calendar for punctual execution of proposals, reports, trips to the field, and other forms of engagement.
	Leverage VisionSpring&rsquo;s Salesforce customer relationship management system to track written communications, meetings, submissions, and gift processing, with strict attention to data integrity.
	Process incoming donations (via check and online giving) and ensure all donors receive timely acknowledgement.


Strategic Planning:


	Actively participate in departmental strategy development and execution.
	Work with the Africa team and Global development team to design and implement annual business development plans and budgets.
	Report on key metrics to the VisionSpring executive leadership and other stakeholders.


&nbsp;Job Requirements


	10 - 15 years of professional experience.
	Demonstrated success managing a portfolio of funders with increasing responsibility and revenue performance.
	Prior experience with relationship management, including working with diverse and sophisticated thinkers.
	Prior experience with programmatically complex proposal writing (preferably international development proposals focused on Africa), pitch deck development, or business plan writing.
	Demonstrated interest in social enterprise models and desire to learn how to articulate and share VisionSpring&rsquo;s unique approach.
	Extraordinary follow-through, attention to detail and deadlines, and a strong personal sense of accountability.
	Must meet the highest standards of ethics, with a demonstrated capacity for discretion and for handling sensitive information with care.
	A strong commitment to respectful collaboration with colleagues from diverse backgrounds.
	Experience developing budgets.
	Aptitude in Microsoft Office, especially Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
	The ability to travel 30-40% of your time, across Nigeria and occasionally across Africa
	Experience synthesizing program updates from to generate evocative and compelling reports.
	Proficiency with CRMs such as Salesforce.


About You:


	You love generating funds for great missions.
	You enjoy the teamwork and project management associated with developing (and managing) large, programmatically complex grant proposals for corporations, foundations and governments.
	You are deeply curious about social enterprise models, field operations, and the experience of partners and customers. That makes you an exceptional organizational ambassador.
	You view funders as integral partners and foster rich relationships with donors that are much more than transactional.
	You shine when communicating with funders, derive satisfaction in finding a shared agenda with others, are upbeat, and can get others excited about what they are going to do.
	You enjoy creating structure, have exceptional focus and follow-through, and prioritize before you act.
	You operate with integrity; you take pride in going above and beyond even when no one is watching. Nothing feels better than exceeding fundraising targets.


Compensation


	Salary range is based on experience,&nbsp;&nbsp;education and location.
	We offer comprehensive benefits, including health insurance and generous vacation time.


Important Information and Notice


	We want to know more than just what you have done. We want to know who you are. What are the passions and talents that draw you to this role?
	Please update your CV/r&eacute;sum&eacute; to include a phrase or sentence describing the business of each organization where you have worked. Applications without this information are disadvantaged in the process.
	Please submit a complete application including a thoughtful cover letter and CV/r&eacute;sum&eacute;.
	Please note, only candidates invited to interview will be contacted.
	At VisionSpring, diversity, equity, and inclusion are directly aligned with our core value to advance equity. Our mission is predicated on removing a fundamental barrier to full economic and social participation&mdash;uncorrected blurry vision. The values that underpin our mission energize our commitment to a diverse and inclusive team.
	A diverse team also allows VisionSpring to approach problems with nuance and develop unique insights that drive an outsized positive impact in the world. We actively recruit, develop, and retain talented people from diverse backgrounds and support everyone in being exactly who they are. VisionSpring is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes people from all experiences, abilities, and perspectives to apply.

Sunchi Integrated Farms was fully incorporated as a limited liability company by the Nigerian Cooperate Affairs Commission on 5th December, 2013 under Nigerian Company and Allied Matters Act of 1990 obtaining Certificate of Incorporation No. RC.846601
Sunchi Integrated Farms Ltd (SIFL) is an associate of E. Sunny Vespa International Companies Limited.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR &amp; Admin Manager

Job Location: Emene, Enugu
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The HR Manager at Sunchi Integrated Farms will play a critical role in shaping and leading human resource strategies to support organizational goals.
	With a focus on developing a positive work environment, you will oversee all HR functions, including talent acquisition, employee engagement, performance management, compliance, and employee relations.
	You will provide both strategic and operational HR support to ensure efficient implementation of policies, employee satisfaction, and organizational growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute effective recruiting strategies to attract and retain top talent in line with business goals.
	Oversee the end-to-end recruitment process, including sourcing, interviewing, selection, onboarding, and induction to ensure new hires have a positive integration experience.
	Manage workforce planning to meet current and future business needs, ensuring positions are filled in a timely manner.
	Foster a positive and inclusive work environment by promoting effective communication, engagement, and consultation with employees at all levels.
	Handle employee grievances, conflicts, and disciplinary actions in compliance with company policy and labor laws.
	Coordinate wellness programs and staff engagement events to improve morale, retention, and overall job satisfaction.
	Implement and manage performance appraisal processes, including annual reviews, performance development plans, and career pathing.
	Collaborate with department heads to establish individual and team KPIs, ensuring alignment with organizational objectives.
	Provide guidance and training to managers on performance management best practices to promote continuous improvement and accountability.
	Develop, implement, and enforce HR policies, procedures, and standards to ensure legal compliance and alignment with the company&#39;s goals.
	Keep abreast of changes in employment laws and regulations, ensuring policies reflect the latest requirements.
	Conduct regular audits of HR practices, documentation, and procedures to maintain high standards and legal compliance.
	Identify and analyze training needs, designing and delivering effective development programs to enhance employee skills, productivity, and career advancement.
	Oversee leadership development, succession planning, and talent management initiatives to build a skilled and adaptable workforce.
	Collaborate with department heads to ensure that L&amp;D initiatives align with business goals and employee career development.
	Oversee payroll, benefits, and compensation policies, ensuring accuracy and adherence to industry standards.
	Manage employee rewards and recognition programs, promoting a culture of achievement and accountability.
	Utilize HRIS to track employee data, monitor key HR metrics, and generate regular reports to guide HR decision-making.
	Maintain employee records and ensure confidentiality of HR data.
	Provide regular reporting on HR activities, metrics, and trends to senior management, offering insights and recommendations for improvement.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field. A Masters degree or HR certification (e.g., CIPM, CIHRM, SHRM, CIPD) is an added advantage.
	Minimum of 5 years of progressive HR experience, including at least 3 years in a managerial role, preferably in agriculture or FMCG.
	Strong knowledge of HR best practices, labor laws, and compliance.
	Proven experience in handling HR operations, employee relations, recruitment, performance management, and learning &amp; development.


Key Competencies:


	Strong leadership and people management skills with the ability to inspire and drive a positive work culture.
	Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, including active listening, negotiation, and conflict resolution.
	Analytical mindset with attention to detail and the ability to interpret HR data for informed decision-making.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and respect for confidentiality.
	Proficiency in HRIS, Microsoft Office, and payroll software.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;

  "description": "GIG Logistics is a member of The GIG Group and prides itself as Nigeria&rsquo;s leading logistics company. Incorporated in 2012 and headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria, GIG Logistics leverages on technology to gain competitive advantage.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Deputy General Manager (DGM), Corporate Sales

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking a dynamic and commercially astute Deputy General Manager (DGM) &ndash; Corporate Sales to lead our Corporate Sales function and drive sustainable revenue growth across key market segments.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and executing corporate sales strategies, expanding strategic client portfolios, strengthening customer relationships, and leading a high-performing sales team to achieve ambitious business objectives.
	The ideal candidate is a strategic leader with a proven track record of delivering revenue growth, managing enterprise accounts, and driving business development within the logistics, transportation, courier, e-commerce, FMCG, financial services, or related industries.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute the Company&#39;s corporate sales strategy to drive revenue growth and market expansion.
	Lead, coach, and manage the Corporate Sales team to achieve individual and departmental sales targets.
	Identify, acquire, and retain high-value corporate clients across key industries.
	Build and maintain strategic relationships with C-level executives, procurement teams, and key decision-makers.
	Drive business development initiatives by identifying new market opportunities, partnerships, and revenue streams.
	Monitor sales performance, pipeline health, and conversion rates, implementing corrective actions where necessary.
	Collaborate with Operations, Customer Experience, Finance, and Marketing teams to deliver exceptional customer service and seamless account management.
	Lead contract negotiations, pricing discussions, and commercial agreements while ensuring profitability.
	Analyze market trends, customer needs, and competitor activities to inform strategic business decisions.
	Prepare and present sales forecasts, business performance reports, and strategic recommendations to Executive Management.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, governance standards, and commercial best practices.
	Champion a high-performance sales culture focused on accountability, innovation, and customer satisfaction.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, Management, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in Corporate Sales, Business Development, or Key Account Management, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role.
	Demonstrated success in delivering significant revenue growth and managing large corporate accounts.
	Strong knowledge of B2B sales, strategic account management, contract negotiation, and commercial planning.
	Proven experience leading and developing high-performing sales teams.
	Excellent leadership, negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong analytical and financial acumen, with the ability to interpret sales data and make strategic decisions.
	Proficiency in CRM platforms and Microsoft Office Suite.
	A postgraduate qualification or professional certification in Sales, Marketing, or Business Management (e.g., MBA, CIM, ISM, NIMN) will be an added advantage.


Preferred Experience:


	Candidates with prior experience in logistics, transportation, courier services, supply chain, e-commerce, FMCG, financial services, telecommunications, or other B2B service-driven organizations will be highly preferred.


Key Competencies:


	Strategic Sales Leadership
	Corporate &amp; Enterprise Sales
	Business Development
	Key Account Management
	Revenue Growth &amp; Profitability
	Commercial Negotiation
	Customer Relationship Management
	Market Intelligence &amp; Competitive Analysis
	Leadership &amp; People Development
	Financial &amp; Business Acumen
	Strategic Thinking &amp; Decision-Making
	Executive Communication &amp; Stakeholder Management.

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a dynamic and commercially astute Deputy General Manager (DGM) – Corporate Sales to lead our Corporate Sales function and drive sustainable revenue growth across key market segments.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and executing corporate sales strategies, expanding strategic client portfolios, strengthening customer relationships, and leading a high-performing sales team to achieve ambitious business objectives.
	The ideal candidate is a strategic leader with a proven track record of delivering revenue growth, managing enterprise accounts, and driving business development within the logistics, transportation, courier, e-commerce, FMCG, financial services, or related industries.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute the Company's corporate sales strategy to drive revenue growth and market expansion.
	Lead, coach, and manage the Corporate Sales team to achieve individual and departmental sales targets.
	Identify, acquire, and retain high-value corporate clients across key industries.
	Build and maintain strategic relationships with C-level executives, procurement teams, and key decision-makers.
	Drive business development initiatives by identifying new market opportunities, partnerships, and revenue streams.
	Monitor sales performance, pipeline health, and conversion rates, implementing corrective actions where necessary.
	Collaborate with Operations, Customer Experience, Finance, and Marketing teams to deliver exceptional customer service and seamless account management.
	Lead contract negotiations, pricing discussions, and commercial agreements while ensuring profitability.
	Analyze market trends, customer needs, and competitor activities to inform strategic business decisions.
	Prepare and present sales forecasts, business performance reports, and strategic recommendations to Executive Management.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, governance standards, and commercial best practices.
	Champion a high-performance sales culture focused on accountability, innovation, and customer satisfaction.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, Management, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in Corporate Sales, Business Development, or Key Account Management, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role.
	Demonstrated success in delivering significant revenue growth and managing large corporate accounts.
	Strong knowledge of B2B sales, strategic account management, contract negotiation, and commercial planning.
	Proven experience leading and developing high-performing sales teams.
	Excellent leadership, negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong analytical and financial acumen, with the ability to interpret sales data and make strategic decisions.
	Proficiency in CRM platforms and Microsoft Office Suite.
	A postgraduate qualification or professional certification in Sales, Marketing, or Business Management (e.g., MBA, CIM, ISM, NIMN) will be an added advantage.


Preferred Experience:


	Candidates with prior experience in logistics, transportation, courier services, supply chain, e-commerce, FMCG, financial services, telecommunications, or other B2B service-driven organizations will be highly preferred.


Key Competencies:


	Strategic Sales Leadership
	Corporate & Enterprise Sales
	Business Development
	Key Account Management
	Revenue Growth & Profitability
	Commercial Negotiation
	Customer Relationship Management
	Market Intelligence & Competitive Analysis
	Leadership & People Development
	Financial & Business Acumen
	Strategic Thinking & Decision-Making
	Executive Communication & Stakeholder Management.

Account Executive - Commercial Sales Job at Dragnet Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Dragnet Solutions Limited is a firm that specializes in the design, development and implementation of people screening solutions that can be deployed in a variety of uses covering both the workplace and the educational arena.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Executive - Commercial Sales

Job Location: Remote

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking Account Executives to join our Commercial/Sales team.
	The Account Executive will be responsible for managing assigned client accounts, identifying new business opportunities, following up on leads, preparing proposals, and supporting the conversion of opportunities into revenue.
	This role will support Dragnet’s commercial growth across business areas such as recruitment, scholarships, assessments, Achieva, and other enterprise solutions.
	The ideal candidate should have experience in sales, business development, account management, client relationship management, or B2B commercial roles. Direct experience in all Dragnet business areas is not required, but the candidate must be able to learn quickly, engage clients confidently, and follow through on opportunities.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and follow up on new business opportunities.
	Manage assigned client accounts and prospects.
	Support renewals, upsells, cross-sells, and account expansion opportunities.
	Engage clients to understand their needs, timelines, and decision-making process.
	Prepare and support proposals, quotations, presentations, and commercial documents.
	Follow up on leads, client enquiries, proposals, and pending decisions.
	Maintain an active sales pipeline with clear next steps and expected timelines.
	Schedule and attend client meetings, presentations, and follow-up discussions.
	Work with internal teams to ensure client requirements are properly understood and delivered.
	Provide regular updates on opportunities, client engagements, proposals, and blockers.
	Support revenue growth across assigned products, services, and client segments.


Qualifications & Skills


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Social Sciences, or a related field.


Experience:


	3–5 years of experience in sales, business development, account management, client relationship management, or a related commercial role.
	Experience in B2B sales, enterprise sales, HR solutions, recruitment, assessments, training, technology, education, or professional services will be an advantage.
	Prior experience managing corporate clients or sales accounts is preferred.


Skills Requirements:


	Strong sales and client relationship management skills.
	Good communication, presentation, and negotiation skills.
	Strong follow-up and attention to detail.
	Ability to identify client needs and match them to relevant solutions.
	Ability to manage multiple clients, opportunities, and deadlines.
	Good understanding of sales pipeline tracking and reporting.
	Target-driven mindset and willingness to take ownership of assigned accounts.
	Ability to work well with internal teams to move opportunities forward.
	Confidence in engaging corporate clients and decision-makers.


Remuneration and Benefits


	Competitive salary package
	Call and data allowance
	Remote work flexibility
	Free access to Udemy courses
	Opportunities for professional growth and impact.


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
